channel.title: Posts on John Wyles dot github dot io
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on John Wyles dot github dot io

item.title: Setting Up Raspberry Pi With an OLED Display
item.description: I purchased a SSD1351 from Adafruit for $40 and it has just been lying around collecting dust since all of my Arduino projects either didn’t require a display or the project parts I purchased already had one included. That isn’t cheap for something to not have a very dutiful purpose sitting around so I decided to scope out a project. It wasn’t any surprise that I wasn’t the only one that came up with the idea of slapping it into my most resource heavy Raspberry Pi to monitor the system statistics.

item.title: Flashing the Creality Ender 3 with a Raspberry Pi
item.description: Most of the guides on the internet only had instructions for Arduino which I did not have lying around. Instead I had a Raspberry Pi and knew it’s GPIO pinouts could be used but couldn’t really find much on until I found: Flash Bootloader and Install Firmware With Raspberry PI. PLEASE SEE THIS GUIDE FOR ANY TROUBLESHOOTING I have re-written it to succinctly summarize the instructions for flashing the Ender 3 using a Raspberry Pi.

item.title: SSH tricks for port forwarding, multiple GitHub accounts, and using alternative ports
item.description: If you are like me you have a home network that, while you are not home, you would like to access from nearly anywhere in the world. I am currently using an OpenVPN server running on my router with a FreeDNS afraid.org dynamic DNS entry that is perpetually updated with a crontab. As an aside to the main focus of this article I will describe what I found while setting up FreeDNS afraid.

item.title: Setting up Kubernetes and OpenFaaS on a Raspberry Pi cluster using Hypriot
item.description: This guide uses a Hypriot blog entry as a massive starting point but goes further to show the steps I took in order to get my kubernetes cluster of three Raspberry Pi computers running. This guide addresses much of the troubleshooting done to fill the gaps and/or “bugs” on this blog entry and applying it to my setup. Also credit to Alex Ellis' Guide for some of the kubernetes troubleshooting.
